mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling on a 3-month basis, discount rates ease faster than earnings expectations adjust, so S&P 500 prices re-rate upward over the next month. The signal fails if rate cuts occur during acute recession scares that overwhelm valuation support.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are negative in more than 55% of months where FEDFUNDS declines over the prior 3 months, or if rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL inflation acceleration above the recent trend forces tighter policy expectations and higher real discount rates, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the following month. The effect fails when inflation surprises are fully offset by simultaneous growth acceleration.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises faster than its 6-month average trend
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not produce negative 20-day returns at least 60% of the time, or if the average forward return after inflation accelerations is not below unconditional mean by at least 1 standard error.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE combined with slowing PAYEMS growth signals weakening household income and labor demand, which should lower sales expectations and weigh on S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is not active when unemployment is stable and payroll gains remain strong.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition does not outperform either UNRATE-only or PAYEMS-only models, or if SPX forward returns are positive in more than 55% of periods where UNRATE rises and PAYEMS growth slows.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS growth is strong relative to the prior quarter, it indicates resilient consumer spending that supports near-term revenue expectations and should lift S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect fails if retail sales strength is driven by inflation rather than real demand.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth turns positive after a prior slowdown
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS growth episodes fail to deliver positive 20-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the signal disappears after controlling for CPIAUCSL.

mechanism_narrative: An improvement in INDPRO with stable or falling CPIAUCSL signals better supply-side output without an inflation penalty, which should support equity margins and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The mechanism fails when production gains come with simultaneous inflation reacceleration.
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction does not yield higher 60-day forward returns than INDPRO alone, or if episodes of rising INDPRO with rising CPIAUCSL do not underperform by at least 0.5 standard deviations.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST below its recent trend marks housing demand weakness that leads broader cyclical spending and earnings lower, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal fails if mortgage-sensitive weakness is isolated and does not spill into labor or retail activity.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 3-month moving average by more than 5%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered housing downturns do not produce negative 20-day SPX returns in at least 60% of cases, or if the average forward return is not below baseline by a statistically meaningful margin.

mechanism_narrative: In a rising-unemployment regime, a simultaneous decline in PAYEMS and INDPRO identifies broad cyclical contraction, which should keep S&P 500 returns negative over the next 5 trading days. The effect fails when unemployment rises but payrolls and production remain resilient.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.2pp over 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if the triggered joint deterioration does not outperform single-series versions, or if 5-day returns are positive in more than 55% of recession-scare episodes.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 and RSAFS both strengthen after prior weakness, aggregate demand is accelerating and firms should revise earnings expectations upward, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The signal fails if GDP growth is driven by inventory noise rather than final demand.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint demand-rebound signal does not produce positive 60-day returns more than 55% of the time, or if its rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.
